summary: Iter 34 (Siding Catalog Restructure) backend verification — all 17 pytest cases passed on first run. Catalog correctly splits siding profiles + accessories + Charter Oak soffit into Standard color / Architectural color variants per Howard's updated Alside Excel sheet. Old single-variant names removed; legacy estimates still load; tier prices, AMI part numbers, and the hover-import endpoint all behave as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: catalog_seed.py is 1008 lines — single source of truth for all tier pricing. Consider splitting per-tier price dictionaries into their own modules (tiers/whole_sale.py, tiers/contractor.py, etc.) so future tier edits don't risk merge conflicts on the same giant file.

TIER_PRICES dictionaries have a misspelled key 'Clean up / haul away job debris' (capital C, spaces around slash) vs SECTION_LAYOUT name 'clean up/ haul away job debris' (lowercase c, no spaces). Lookups via .get(n,0) return 0 silently — this is why those rows still cost $0. Not Iter 34-related but worth flagging.

ITEM_AMI is missing entries for the new Standard/Architectural soffit J-Channel variants in /app/backend/routes/catalog.py output? Verified via test: AMI '105118' IS present on '3/4" Soffit J-Channel (Charter Oak) Standard color'. OK — false alarm; tests confirm presence.
/api/admin/tiers correctly rejects missing token (401/403); good defensive auth.

seed_data_creation: Estimate round-trip test creates a TEST_Iter34_<uuid> estimate then deletes it. No persistent test data left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration34_siding_split.py is the canonical regression file for Iter 34 catalog shape. Run it whenever catalog_seed.py is touched. Each tier price assertion uses Charter Oak Standard Clap 4.5".046 as the canary; expand the by_name lookup pattern to validate other variants if future iterations require it.
